resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def findComplement(self, num: int) -> int:
        mask = 1
        while mask <= num:
            mask <<= 1
        return (mask - 1) ^ num
```
solution: `mask = 1\nwhile mask <= num:\n    mask <<= 1\nreturn (mask - 1) ^ num`
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution utilizes bit manipulation to efficiently find the complement. It first calculates a mask with all bits set to 1 up to the length of the input number's binary representation. Then, it XORs the mask with the input number, effectively flipping the bits.
code:
def findComplement(self, num: int) -> int:
        mask = 1
        while mask <= num:
            mask <<= 1
        return (mask - 1) ^ num
```
solution: `mask = 1\nwhile mask <= num:\n    mask <<= 1\nreturn (mask - 1) ^ num`
